### Post by stuppaz on 2007-07-05

Thanks for your help.

---

### Post by deepclutch on 2007-07-05
ur net is connected.u can try posting here the output of:
```
 route -n
```
also post the o/p of  "ifconfig" while gnome-ppp connects
and if ur internet interface is ppp0,try:
```
sudo route add default ppp0
```
btwn if ur not afraid of terminal,cant u use "pon" to connect(which gnome-ppp too uses).

---

### Post by deepclutch on 2007-07-05
I hope this is a dialup or gprs connection involving cellphone.U got 2 lancards?
OK.Now do a ```
 sudo route add default ppp0
```
^and try browsing or ping.does it works? and ur route table seems weird.did u have a adsl connection too ?

### Post by stuppaz on 2007-07-05

sudo route add default ppp0

it worked !!

But this means that everytime I have to run command route add ?
Sorry but I am newby on linux ...

### Post by deepclutch on 2007-07-05
I dont know much about kernel modules and its purposes.but u can press the module to auto load either using a nifty tool called "modconf" apt-get it if u want or add the module name in /etc/modules.

u can know its work from "modinfo modlname" or kernel documentaion.

### Post by deepclutch on 2007-07-05
this may be attributed to ur ethernet link.perhaps "route -n" gives some clue.
eth0 is shown in routing table and in ifconfig shows as eth1-some udev rules?
and here is a good hw2 on getting ppp0 as default route.try
[http://linux-ip.net/html/basic-changing.html](http://linux-ip.net/html/basic-changing.html)

---

### Post by stuppaz on 2007-07-05
sorry, but what you mean about: udev rules ?

---

### Post by deepclutch on 2007-07-05
udev is device file manager.
it is weird to set route default as ppp0,means ur lan card are assuming as default route.do try below hw2:
[http://linux.omnipotent.net/article.php?article_id=4173](http://linux.omnipotent.net/article.php?article_id=4173)
